what_it_does: Defines a content registry that maps story ids to story JSON files with subject, line, and note metadata, alongside meta statements about keeping authored content outside dna/ and pointing to story sources instead of copying text.
observations: The file has a meta block with version, identity statement, and a registry law.
The stories object contains two entries: workshop and grown-1781331418282.
Each story entry points to a content/stories/*.json file and includes subject, line, and note fields.
The meta text states that stories are tree-of-nodes artifacts and pieces hold addresses into them.
The registry explicitly describes itself as outside dna/ because DNA is identity rather than content.
standouts: The registry's stated rule is points, never copies.
Story entries combine machine-addressable file paths with narrative one-line tellings.
The file distinguishes authored content from identity/DNA content.
conventions: Uses id-keyed objects for story registry entries.
Uses file, subject, line, and note as repeated per-story fields.
Uses narrative explanatory strings inside metadata rather than only terse schema fields.
Keeps content references as relative paths under content/stories/.
concerns: The note field carries mixed kinds of information, including provenance, migration history, and timestamp-like data.
The timestamp in grown-1781331418282 appears only inside free text, not as a structured field.
notes: This file is the authored-content index: story ids point to their source story files and carry short descriptive metadata.
questions: Should note remain free text, or should provenance and kept time be structured separately?
Are story ids expected to be stable external addresses?
Is the registry limited to stories, or will other authored content types appear here later?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it presents a registry that maps story ids to source files and short tellings while avoiding copied story text.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make authored story content discoverable by stable ids while preserving source-file ownership of the text and separating content records from dna identity records.
novelty: The file blends registry mechanics with domain language such as AUTHORED half, DNA, raw seams, and kept work.
contribution: Contributes a lightweight authored-content ledger that maps story ids to story artifact paths and preserves compact descriptive metadata for each story without embedding the story text.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON content registry for counterpart-design that maps story ids to content story files with subject, line, note, and meta rules about pointing to sources instead of copying text.
intention_for_embedding: It was for keeping authored story artifacts addressable and discoverable while separating content from identity/DNA records.
